commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared self-prefix stripping helper to model-id normalization and tests Google, XAI, OpenAI, Vercel AI Gateway, and fallback candidate behavior.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +22. Total +35 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main retains self-prefixed model ids for the reported non-Anthropic provider branches, and the fallback/model lookup paths consume that shared normalization result.
commands.review_followup: - No repair lane is needed because the patch has no blocking review findings and should proceed through normal maintainer review and CI.

target.title: fix(models): strip remaining provider self prefixes
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes native model normalization for the remaining self-prefixed providers called out in #88770: Google variants, xAI, OpenAI, and Vercel AI Gateway.
- Adds a shared self-prefix strip helper and keeps existing provider-specific alias behavior intact.
- Extends regression coverage for provider catalog normalization and fallback candidate normalization so `provider/provider-model` shapes do not leak through the chain.

## Linked context

Closes #88770

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: self-prefixed native model ids were retained for several providers, producing doubled model refs such as `google/google/gemini-2.0-flash` in downstream lookup paths.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on the current `main` base for this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `corepack pnpm exec tsx -e "import { normalizeStaticProviderModelIdWithPolicies } from './packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.ts'; for (const [p,m] of [['google','google/gemini-2.0-flash'],['xai','xai/grok-4-fast'],['openai','openai/gpt-5.4'],['vercel-ai-gateway','vercel-ai-gateway/opus-4.6']]) console.log(p, m, '=>', normalizeStaticProviderModelIdWithPolicies(p,m));"`
- Evidence after fix:
  - `google google/gemini-2.0-flash => gemini-2.0-flash`
  - `xai xai/grok-4-fast => grok-4-fast`
  - `openai openai/gpt-5.4 => gpt-5.4`
  - `vercel-ai-gateway vercel-ai-gateway/opus-4.6 => anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`
- Observed result after fix: native providers strip only their own redundant provider prefix before catalog/fallback lookup, while provider-specific aliases still apply.
- What was not tested: no live provider calls were made.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a normalization bug, so the real proof is the exported normalization helper output plus focused regression tests; no secrets or runtime provider credentials were used.
- Before evidence: the same helper retained `google/gemini-2.0-flash`, `xai/grok-4-fast`, `openai/gpt-5.4`, and `vercel-ai-gateway/opus-4.6` before this patch.

## Tests and validation

- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.ts packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed: 2 Vitest shards, 79 tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: model id normalization for provider-prefixed values.
- Mitigation: the patch only strips a prefix matching the current provider branch, leaves gateway/cross-provider model refs alone, and adds focused regression coverage.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review. Nothing is waiting on the author beyond CI/reviewer feedback.
target.author: charles-openclaw
